{"key_event":"Fascist consolidation and Dervish defeat","reasoning":"The destruction of the Dervish movement, Italian suppression of Hobyo and Majeerteen, taxation, disarmament, courts, and plantation expansion raised colonial state capacity substantially, though British Somaliland remained lightly administered and overall authority was still uneven. Society power fell because coordinated anti-colonial mobilization and indigenous sultanates were broken, but clan institutions and indirect-rule elders still retained meaningful local capacity, especially in the British zone.","society_change":-1.0,"society_power":4.5,"state_change":1.5,"state_power":4.5}
